notes:
I was getting more performance over the native port. On medium settings i was getting locked 60 at 1080p resolution. On the native port i was getting around 40 ish fps on medium and that also on 900p. 
issues with the non native version:
Changing the resolution crashes the game. The game has trilinear vysnc on by default. Trying to change it causes the game to crash. Also the game sometimes gets a 30 fps lock even when it is capable of getting a much higher fps. I have to go into a room and then again the fps gets back to 60 fps lock. It might be something with the trilinear vsync. Overall the game runs really smooth and runs better than the native port. 
If you have the native game installed then it will only require a download of 400 mb to run it on proton.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes: Tested with the per-title override to make it use Steam Play instead of running natively. The game fails to launch on both 3.16-7 Beta and 3.7-8.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes: Game runs perfectly under Proton 3.16-7 Beta.  Much improved for amdgpu users where it would fail to play due to the OpenGL issue on the native port.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes:
I could never get in-home streaming to work with the native version, but it works fine with Steam Play. Graphics look good and smooth, keyboard & mouse control are good, audio sounds right. I did not try playing with a controller.

The game crashes when attempting to change video resolution or windowed/fullscreen mode, but these can be changed manually by editing ~/.local/share/Steam/SteamApps/compatdata/373420/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/Larian\ Studios/Divinity\ Original\ Sin\ Enhanced\ Edition/graphicSettings.lsx.

To get your Linux saved games to work in Steam Play, copy or symlink ~/Larian\ Studios/Divinity\ Original\ Sin\ Enhanced\ Edition/PlayerProfiles into ~/.local/share/Steam/SteamApps/compatdata/373420/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/Larian\ Studios/Divinity\ Original\ Sin\ Enhanced\ Edition/
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes: The native version doesn't run on AMD cards, an issue the developers refuse to fix. With Proton the game seems to run perfectly based on a few minutes of play.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes:
Ran into no issues so far. I had played 30 hours on Windows and continued my cloud synced save and played another hour or two with no noticeable differences with Proton. Everything worked perfectly, even streaming to a Steam Link.

The native version does not work, needed to use Proton otherwise as other people have reported it will error out with an OpenGL error with an AMD gpu the second you launch the game.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes: Native game does not work with AMD cards. Runs perfectly with Proton, only issue I've discovered is that the game crashes if you try to change the V-sync setting. Played over 50 hours so far.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes:
AMD users with Mesa drivers need to follow the guide in this link to fix the game. Very easy to do, just needs the use of a text editor, game runs perfectly afterwards!

notes: Native Linux client refused to start. With Proton it just worked.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes: no sound in native version. proton runs fine, but every now and then game goes to black screen which can be fixed by alt+tab
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition

notes:
Native version runs without sound by default, but tweaking PulseAudio made that work. Tweak was to select headphones as the output source because starting the game unselected all audio outputs for whatever reason. Everything else was fine.

Proton wasn't necessary.
title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition
